content:
# Project Structure

## Directory Organization

```
hextrackr/
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ app/                          # Application root
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ config/                   # Server & feature configuration
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ database.js           # SQLite connection config
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ importConfig.js       # CSV import field mappings
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ logging.config.json   # Logging categories & levels
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ middleware.js          # Express middleware setup
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ server.js             # Server bootstrap config
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ websocket.js          # Socket.IO configuration
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ controllers/              # Request handlers (14 controllers)
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ auditLogController.js
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ authController.js
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ backupController.js
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ ciscoController.js
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ docsController.js
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ hostnameController.js
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ importController.js
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ kevController.js
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ locationController.js
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ paloAltoController.js
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ preferencesController.js
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ templateController.js
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ ticketController.js
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ vulnerabilityController.js
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ data/                     # SQLite database (Docker volume mount)
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ dev-docs-html/            # JSDoc-generated API reference (build artifact)
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ middleware/               # Express middleware (5 files)
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ auth.js               # Session authentication
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ errorHandler.js       # Global error handler
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ logging.js            # Request logging
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ security.js           # Helmet, CSRF, rate limiting
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ validation.js         # Input validation
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ modules/                  # Shared domain modules
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ public/                   # Static frontend assets (Express static root)
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ config/               # Client-side configuration
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ docs-html/            # Generated documentation HTML (build artifact)
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ docs-source/          # Markdown documentation source (editable)
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ changelog/versions/  # Per-version changelog files
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ fonts/                # Web fonts
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ scripts/              # Frontend JavaScript files
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ styles/               # CSS stylesheets
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ uploads/              # User file uploads
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ vendor/               # Third-party frontend libraries
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ index.html            # Main dashboard SPA
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ login.html            # Login page
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ tickets.html          # Ticketing Bridge page
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ vulnerabilities.html  # Vulnerability management page
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ server.js             # Express server entry point
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ routes/                   # Route modules (18 files)
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ scripts/                  # Build/utility scripts
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ services/                 # Business logic (20+ service files)
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ uploads/                  # Server-side upload staging
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ utils/                    # Shared utilities
‚îÇ       ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ constants.js          # Application-wide constants
‚îÇ       ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ helpers.js            # General helper functions
‚îÇ       ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ httpHelpers.js        # HTTP request utilities
‚îÇ       ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ PathValidator.js      # File path security validation
‚îÇ       ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ ProgressTracker.js    # Import progress tracking
‚îÇ       ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ seedEmailTemplates.js # Default email template data
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ docs/                         # Developer documentation (markdown)
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ devops/                       # Version lock, deployment config
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ backups/                      # Database backups (git-ignored)
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ .codacy/                      # Codacy quality gate configuration
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ .spec-workflow/               # Spec workflow steering & specs
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ docker-compose.yml            # Container orchestration
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ Dockerfile                    # Container build definition
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ package.json                  # Dependencies & scripts
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ eslint.config.mjs             # ESLint v9 flat config
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ .stylelintrc.json             # Stylelint CSS rules
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ .markdownlint.json            # Markdown lint rules
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ jsdoc.dev.json                # JSDoc generation config
```

## Naming Conventions

### Files

- **Controllers**: `camelCase` with `Controller` suffix (e.g., `ticketController.js`)
- **Services**: `camelCase` with `Service` suffix (e.g., `ticketService.js`)
- **Routes**: `kebab-case` or single-word (e.g., `palo-alto.js`, `tickets.js`)
- **Middleware**: `camelCase` single-word (e.g., `auth.js`, `security.js`)
- **Utilities**: `camelCase` or `PascalCase` for classes (e.g., `helpers.js`, `PathValidator.js`)
- **Backup files**: `.bak` suffix ‚Äî retained for rollback reference, not loaded at runtime

### Code

- **Classes**: `PascalCase` (e.g., `DatabaseService`, `PathValidator`)
- **Functions/Methods**: `camelCase` (e.g., `getVulnerabilities`, `parseCsvRow`)
- **Constants**: `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` (e.g., `CACHE_TTL`, `MAX_RETRIES`)
- **Variables**: `camelCase` (e.g., `ticketData`, `advisoryList`)
- **Database columns**: `snake_case` (e.g., `created_at`, `kev_status`)

## Import Patterns

### Import Order

1. Node.js built-in modules (`path`, `fs`, `crypto`)
2. External npm dependencies (`express`, `better-sqlite3`)
3. Internal config (`../config/database`)
4. Internal services (`../services/databaseService`)
5. Internal utilities (`../utils/helpers`)

### Module Organization

- CommonJS throughout (`require`/`module.exports`)
- Relative imports within the `app/` tree
- Services instantiated as singletons ‚Äî one instance shared across the application
- Controllers receive service instances via constructor or direct require

## Code Structure Patterns

### Service Pattern

```
class FooService {
    constructor() {
        this.db = null;        // Set during init
        this._log = null;      // LoggingService reference
    }

    initialize(db, loggingService) {
        this.db = db;
        this._log = loggingService;
    }

    // Public methods
    async getFoo(id) { ... }

    // Private helpers prefixed with _
    _validateFoo(data) { ... }
}

module.exports = new FooService();  // Singleton export
```

### Controller Pattern

```
// Controllers are plain objects with handler methods
const fooController = {
    async getAll(req, res) {
        try {
            const data = await fooService.getAll();
            res.json({ success: true, data });
        } catch (error) {
            res.status(500).json({ success: false, error: error.message });
        }
    }
};
```

### Route Pattern

```
const express = require("express");
const router = express.Router();
const { requireAuth } = require("../middleware/auth");

router.get("/", requireAuth, fooController.getAll);

module.exports = router;
```

### SQLite Callback `this` Context

When using `sqlite3` (async driver) with `db.run()` callbacks, capture `const self = this` before the callback. Regular function callbacks rebind `this` to the SQLite Statement object (`this.lastID`, `this.changes`), so service methods like `this._log()` become undefined. Arrow functions would fix `this` but break access to Statement properties.

## Code Organization Principles

1. **Single Responsibility**: Each service owns one domain (tickets, vulnerabilities, auth, etc.)
2. **Layered Architecture**: Routes ‚Üí Controllers ‚Üí Services ‚Üí Database. No layer-skipping.
3. **Singleton Services**: Services are instantiated once and initialized with shared dependencies (db, logger)
4. **Frontend Simplicity**: Vanilla JS with direct DOM manipulation ‚Äî no virtual DOM, no state management library

## Module Boundaries

- **Backend vs Frontend**: Backend in `app/` (Node.js), frontend in `app/public/` (browser JS). No shared code between them.
- **Services vs Controllers**: Services contain business logic and database access. Controllers handle HTTP concerns only (parsing request, formatting response).
- **Vendor Integrations**: Each vendor (Cisco, Palo Alto, CISA KEV) has its own dedicated service + controller + route module.
- **Documentation**: Source in `docs-source/` (editable markdown), output in `docs-html/` (generated HTML, never edit directly).

## Code Size Guidelines

- **File size**: Services can be large (500-1000+ lines) due to comprehensive domain logic. Keep controllers lean (under 200 lines).
- **Function size**: Prefer functions under 50 lines. Extract helpers for complex logic.
- **Nesting depth**: Maximum 3 levels of nesting. Use early returns to reduce depth.

## Documentation Standards

- All public service methods should have JSDoc comments
- Complex business logic should include inline comments explaining "why"
- Developer docs in `/docs/` folder (markdown)
- User docs in `app/public/docs-source/` (markdown, rendered to HTML)
- API reference auto-generated via JSDoc to `app/dev-docs-html/`
